van gogh.example_clue: This troubled artist considered " A Wheat Field with Cypresses " one of his best landscapes

brazil.example_clue: Two Portuguese planters & two Black slaves appear in the first European artwork to depict this country from 1637

claude monet.example_clue: In 1873, he painted a field of poppies near Argenteuil

yosemite.example_clue: Albert Bierstadt's landscape of this iconic American valley shows the Sun's rays bathing Sentinel Rock

thomas gainsborough.example_clue: Combining a double portrait & a landscape, " Mr. and Mrs. Andrews " is by this Englishman also famous for a "Blue" portrait

taggers.example_clue: ( Kareem Rahma presents the clue. ) In a 1974 essay, Norman Mailer compared these subway graffiti artists with their own signatures to Boticelli & Michelangelo

coming to america.example_clue: ( Kareem Rahma presents the clue. ) Underground can be a romantic place, as in this film when Eddie Murphy as Prince Akeem Joffer proposes to Lisa on the subway; one filming location was Brooklyn's Hoyt-Schermerhorn Station

(gene) hackman.example_clue: ( Kareem Rahma presents the clue. ) Including a scene where he gets outfoxed on the subway platform, the role of Popeye Doyle in "The French Connection" won an Oscar for this man who prided himself on being a New York actor

the colosseum.example_clue: ( Kareem Rahma presents the clue. ) Rome is building a new subway line to help with the city's "eternal" traffic; you can see ancient artifacts in the station under this vast landmark begun by Emperor Vespasian

take the a train.example_clue: ( Kareem Rahma presents the clue. ) If you want to get from the Transit Museum to Duke Ellington's longtime home at 935 St. Nicholas Avenue in Harlem, all I can tell you is to do this -- Duke's signature tune

streetlights (lampposts).example_clue: A landmark outside a landmark, "Urban Light" at LACMA is made up of 202 cast-iron these, refurbished by artist Chris Burden

falstaff.example_clue: The Dorothy Chandler Pavilion is home to the L.A. Opera, which in 2026 staged Verdi's comic opera named for this Shakespeare wooer

the newman family.example_clue: A USC recital hall is named for 9-time Oscar-winning composer Alfred of this family, father of Thomas & David

walt disney.example_clue: Designed by Frank Gehry & completed in 2003, this Concert Hall in downtown L.A. has been called an acoustical nirvana

(armand) hammer.example_clue: This former chairman of Occidental Petroleum founded an L.A. museum that opened in 1990, just 2 weeks before he died

dante.example_clue: The coup de grace of "The Gates of Hell" is Rodin's "The Thinker", a representation of this Italian writer

pop art.example_clue: An Obie & Emmy winner, Rosalyn Drexler was also a formidable artist in this movement of Warhol & Marisol

david.example_clue: While the original of this Michelangelo statue is indoors at Florence's Accademia Gallery, there are 2 outdoor copies nearby

velázquez.example_clue: Born in Seville, this 17th c. artist traveled to Italy to create paintings like " Vulcan's Forge "

(dale) chihuly.example_clue: The Glasshouse in Seattle is a one-of-a-kind structure holding a 100-foot sculpture by this artist

an igloo.example_clue: Everett, Pennsylvania is home to an ice cream shop named for these domed structures but actually shaped like a sundae

a hot dog.example_clue: Around since 1946, west Hollywood's Tail o' the Pup is shaped like this food item it serves (& no, it's not a sandwich)

route 66.example_clue: The rooms at the Wigwam Village Motel in Hollbrook, Arizona, on this historic road, are actually shaped like teepees

a shell.example_clue: Winston-Salem, North Carolina has the last gas station shaped like this, also the name of an oil company

a howdah.example_clue: Henry Ford is among the notables who visited Lucy the Elephant in New Jersey & climbed the 130 stairs to this seat on her back
